items.title: Logistic regression is convex in the weights
items.scenario: Training logistic regression minimizes average cross-entropy in the weight vector w. The loss is convex in w, so any critical point found by gradient descent is the global optimum (for the given data and regularization).
items.explanation: This is a rare ML setting with a provable global minimum—contrasts sharply with deep networks where the same optimizer has no such guarantee.

items.title: Two valleys in a neural loss surface
items.scenario: Two different random initializations for the same MLP on MNIST converge to different training losses and slightly different test accuracy, even with identical hyperparameters.
items.explanation: Non-convexity creates multiple basins; SGD follows different paths and can settle in different local minima or wide flat regions.

items.title: Saddle in a quadratic toy model
items.scenario: The function f(x, y) = x^2 - y^2 has gradient zero at (0, 0) but is a saddle: it decreases along y and increases along x.
items.explanation: Gradient descent from a point with small x and positive y can move away from the origin even though the gradient vanishes there—illustrating why zero gradient does not imply a minimum.
